_metadata.description: Dosing bands for Batch 5: GI + Skin + ENT + Surgical + Other (183 drugs)

Omeprazole.duration_note: Review after 4 weeks; max 8 weeks initial course
Omeprazole.notes: May increase to 3 mg/kg OD (max 20 mg) if inadequate response. Give before food. Capsule may be opened and mixed with apple juice or yoghurt.

Omeprazole.duration_note: Review after 4 weeks; max 8 weeks initial course
Omeprazole.notes: 10-20 kg: 10 mg OD; >20 kg: 20 mg OD. May increase to 40 mg OD for erosive esophagitis. Give before breakfast.

Omeprazole.duration_note: 4-8 weeks; step down to lowest effective dose
Omeprazole.notes: 20 mg OD for GERD; 40 mg OD for erosive esophagitis. Give 30 min before breakfast.

Ondansetron.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat up to 3 doses in 24h if needed (q8h)
Ondansetron.notes: Use with caution <6 months (slower clearance). Oral route preferred for gastroenteritis. Single dose usually sufficient. Monitor QTc if repeat dosing.
8-15 kg: 2 mg; 15-30 kg: 4 mg; >30 kg: 4-8 mg. PO/IV/IM. For CINV: 0.15 mg/kg q4h x3 doses (max 16 mg/dose).

Ondansetron.duration_note: CINV: start 30 min before chemo; continue 1-2 days post
Ondansetron.notes: IV: 0.1 mg/kg single dose (max 4 mg) for PONV. CINV: 0.15 mg/kg IV (max 16 mg) q4-8h x 3 doses then q8h.

PANTOPRAZOLE.duration_note: Up to 8 weeks; safety not established <5 yr
PANTOPRAZOLE.notes: 15-<40 kg: 20 mg OD; >=40 kg: 40 mg OD. IV: 0.8-1 mg/kg OD (max 40 mg). Give before breakfast.
40 mg OD for erosive esophagitis; 20 mg OD for maintenance. Give before breakfast.

Lansoprazole.duration_note: Up to 12 weeks for erosive esophagitis
Lansoprazole.notes: <=30 kg: 15 mg OD; >30 kg: 30 mg OD. Capsule may be opened and sprinkled on soft food. Give before food.

Lansoprazole.duration_note: 4-8 weeks; step down to lowest effective dose
Lansoprazole.notes: 30 mg OD for erosive esophagitis; 15 mg OD for maintenance GERD.

Esomeprazole.duration_note: Up to 6 weeks in infants 1-11 months
Esomeprazole.notes: 3.5-<5 kg: 2.5 mg OD; 5-<7.5 kg: 5 mg OD; >=7.5 kg: 10 mg OD. Granule sachet in water.

Esomeprazole.duration_note: Up to 8 weeks for erosive esophagitis
Esomeprazole.notes: 10-<20 kg: 10 mg OD; >=20 kg: 10-20 mg OD. Give before breakfast.
20 mg OD GERD; 40 mg OD erosive esophagitis.

Famotidine.notes: <3 months: 0.5 mg/kg OD; 3-12 months: 0.5 mg/kg BD. Use cautiously in neonates — risk of NEC.

Famotidine.duration_note: 2-4 weeks for GERD; up to 8 weeks for ulcer
Famotidine.notes: 0.5 mg/kg BD (max 40 mg/day). IV: 0.25 mg/kg q12h (max 20 mg/dose).

Ranitidine/Famotidine.indication: GERD / Peptic ulcer — Famotidine preferred (ranitidine withdrawn in many markets)
Ranitidine/Famotidine.duration_note: Famotidine dosing shown; ranitidine withdrawn due to NDMA contamination
Ranitidine/Famotidine.notes: Ranitidine withdrawn 2020 (NDMA). Use famotidine 0.5 mg/kg BD (max 40 mg/day) or PPI instead.

Lactulose.duration_note: Titrate to 2-3 soft stools/day; may use long-term
Lactulose.notes: 1-3 mL/kg/day divided BD. Titrate to produce 2-3 soft stools daily. Safe for long-term use.

Lactulose.duration_note: Long-term use acceptable; titrate to effect
Lactulose.notes: 1-3 years: 5-10 mL/day; 3-6 years: 10-15 mL/day; 6-12 years: 15-30 mL/day. Adjust to stool consistency.
Higher doses required for hepatic encephalopathy. Enema: 30-50 mL diluted in 100 mL water can also be used.

Racecadotril.duration_note: Max 7 days; stop when 2 normal stools or no stool for 12h
Racecadotril.notes: <9 kg: 10 mg TDS; 9-13 kg: 20 mg TDS; >13 kg: 30 mg TDS. Available as 10 mg, 30 mg sachets. Must be given with ORS.
13-27 kg: 30 mg TDS; >27 kg: 60 mg TDS. ESPGHAN: may be considered for acute diarrhea as adjunct to ORS.

Zinc sulfate.duration_note: 10-14 days (WHO); give even after diarrhea resolves
Zinc sulfate.notes: <6 months: 10 mg OD (half tablet). Dissolve in breast milk or ORS. WHO/UNICEF standard.
6 months to 5 years: 20 mg OD (1 tablet or 5 mL syrup). Give with ORS for 10-14 days.

ORAL REHYDRATION SALTS.duration_note: Give over 4 hours for rehydration; continue maintenance until diarrhea stops
ORAL REHYDRATION SALTS.notes: WHO low-osmolarity ORS: 1 sachet in 1 litre water. Mild dehydration: 50 mL/kg over 4h; Moderate: 100 mL/kg over 4h. Maintenance: 10 mL/kg after each loose stool.
WHO Plan B: 75 mL/kg over 4h for some dehydration. Maintenance: 10 mL/kg per loose stool. Use with zinc supplementation.

PEG 3350 (Polyethylene Glycol).duration_note: May use long-term; titrate to effect
PEG 3350 (Polyethylene Glycol).notes: Start 0.4 g/kg/day, titrate up. Mix powder in water or juice. Safe for long-term use.

PEG 3350 (Polyethylene Glycol).duration_note: Long-term; slow taper over weeks once regular
PEG 3350 (Polyethylene Glycol).notes: 0.2-0.8 g/kg/day maintenance. Target 1-2 soft stools/day. First-line osmotic laxative per ESPGHAN/NASPGHAN.

PEG 3350 (Polyethylene Glycol).duration_note: 3-6 days for disimpaction; then step down to maintenance
PEG 3350 (Polyethylene Glycol).notes: 1-1.5 g/kg/day for 3 days (max 100 g/day). Follow with maintenance dosing. ESPGHAN first-line for disimpaction.

Sucralfate.notes: 40-80 mg/kg/day divided QID. Give 1 hour before meals and at bedtime on empty stomach. Do not give with antacids.

DOMPERIDONE.duration_note: Max 7 days due to cardiac risk (QTc prolongation)
DOMPERIDONE.notes: 0.25 mg/kg TDS before meals (max 0.5 mg/kg/day). EMA restricted to <7 days. Risk of QTc prolongation — avoid with CYP3A4 inhibitors. Not recommended <12 yr in UK/EU.

Metoclopramide.duration_note: Max 5 days. High risk of extrapyramidal reactions in children.
Metoclopramide.notes: 0.1-0.15 mg/kg up to TDS (max 0.5 mg/kg/day). NOT recommended <1 yr. High EPS risk especially in young children. Prefer ondansetron.

DICYCLOMINE.notes: CONTRAINDICATED <6 months (apnea, seizures, death reported). Only for infants >6 months. Use with extreme caution.
>2 years: 5-10 mg TDS-QID. Give 15 min before meals.

Loperamide.duration_note: Max 2 days. CONTRAINDICATED <2 years. Avoid in dysentery.
Loperamide.notes: CONTRAINDICATED <2 years (ileus, CNS depression). Not recommended <3 years (KIDs list). 2-5 yr: 1 mg TDS (max 3 mg/day); 6-8 yr: 2 mg BD (max 4 mg/day); 9-12 yr: 2 mg TDS (max 6 mg/day). Avoid in bloody diarrhea.

BISACODYL.duration_note: Short-term stimulant laxative; not for chronic daily use without specialist guidance
BISACODYL.notes: 4-10 years: 5 mg at bedtime; >10 years: 5-10 mg at bedtime. Suppository: 5-10 mg PR. Swallow tablets whole — do not crush (enteric coated).

SODIUM PICOSULFATE.duration_note: Short-term use; second-line if osmotic laxatives insufficient
SODIUM PICOSULFATE.notes: 1 month-4 years: 2.5-10 mg OD; 4-18 years: 2.5-20 mg OD. Give at bedtime. Liquid formulation preferred in young children.

Desloratadine.duration_note: Can be used long-term for chronic allergic rhinitis
Desloratadine.notes: 6-11 months: 1 mg OD (2 mL syrup); 1-5 years: 1.25 mg OD (2.5 mL syrup). Non-sedating second-generation antihistamine.
2-5 years: 1.25 mg OD; 6-11 years: 2.5 mg OD. Give at same time daily.
>=12 years: 5 mg OD tablet or 10 mL syrup.

Fexofenadine.notes: 6 months to <2 years: 15 mg BD. Non-sedating antihistamine.
2-11 years: 30 mg BD. Truly non-sedating — does not cross BBB.
>=12 years: 60 mg BD or 180 mg OD. Take without food for best absorption.

Hydroxyzine.notes: <6 yr: 50 mg/day divided; >=6 yr: 50-100 mg/day divided. Max 2 mg/kg/day. Sedating — use at bedtime when possible. Risk of QT prolongation at high doses.

Diphenhydramine.duration_note: Short-term; prefer non-sedating antihistamines for chronic use
Diphenhydramine.notes: 1-1.25 mg/kg q6-8h (max 5 mg/kg/day or 300 mg/day). Highly sedating. IV: 1 mg/kg (max 50 mg) for acute allergic reactions. Not recommended <1 year.

Promethazine.notes: CONTRAINDICATED <2 years (fatal respiratory depression). 2-5 years: 5-15 mg/day divided; 5-12 years: 10-25 mg TDS. Deep IM only — NEVER IV (tissue necrosis). Sedating.

Dimenhydrinate.duration_note: Give 30 min before travel for motion sickness
Dimenhydrinate.notes: 2-5 years: 12.5-25 mg q6-8h (max 75 mg/day); 6-12 years: 25-50 mg q6-8h (max 150 mg/day). Not recommended <2 years.

Cyproheptadine.notes: 2-6 years: 2 mg BD-TDS (max 12 mg/day); 7-14 years: 4 mg BD-TDS (max 16 mg/day). 0.25 mg/kg/day alternative. Not for <2 years. Sedating — give at bedtime.

Fluticasone propionate nasal spray.duration_note: Long-term use for seasonal/perennial allergic rhinitis
Fluticasone propionate nasal spray.notes: >=4 years: 1 spray (50 mcg) each nostril OD (100 mcg/day). May increase to 2 sprays each nostril OD (200 mcg/day). Step down to lowest effective dose.

Oxymetazoline.duration_note: MAX 3 days — risk of rebound congestion (rhinitis medicamentosa)
Oxymetazoline.notes: NOT for <6 years. 6-12 years: 0.025% — 1-2 sprays each nostril BD; >12 years: 0.05% — 2-3 sprays each nostril BD. NEVER >3 days.

Olopatadine.duration_note: Use during allergy season; long-term safety established
Olopatadine.notes: >=2 years (0.1%): 1 drop each eye BD; >=2 years (0.2%/0.7%): 1 drop each eye OD. Dual-action: antihistamine + mast cell stabilizer.

Ketotifen 0.025% ophthalmic (OTC).notes: >=3 years: 1 drop each eye q8-12h. OTC. Antihistamine + mast cell stabilizer. Wait 10 min before inserting contact lenses.

Griseofulvin microsize oral.duration_note: 6-8 weeks for tinea capitis; may need 12 weeks
Griseofulvin microsize oral.notes: Microsize: 20-25 mg/kg/day OD (or divided BD). Give with fatty food for better absorption. FDA approved >=2 years. AAP recommends higher doses (20-25 mg/kg).

Terbinafine.duration_note: 4-6 weeks for tinea capitis; 6-12 weeks for onychomycosis
Terbinafine.notes: <20 kg: 62.5 mg OD; 20-40 kg: 125 mg OD; >40 kg: 250 mg OD. More effective than griseofulvin for Trichophyton species. Monitor LFTs.

Itraconazole oral.duration_note: Duration varies by infection: tinea capitis 4-6 wk; systemic 3-6 months
Itraconazole oral.notes: 3-5 mg/kg/day OD for superficial; 5-10 mg/kg/day for systemic. Oral solution preferred (better absorption). TDM recommended — target trough >0.5 mg/L. Children <12 yr often need higher mg/kg doses. Give with food (capsules) or empty stomach (solution).

Voriconazole.duration_note: Duration based on clinical/mycological response; minimum 6 weeks
Voriconazole.notes: 2-<12 yr: IV loading 9 mg/kg q12h day 1, then 8 mg/kg q12h; oral 9 mg/kg q12h (max 350 mg). >=12 yr and >=50 kg: adult dosing (6 mg/kg IV q12h day 1 then 4 mg/kg q12h). TDM essential — target trough 1-5.5 mg/L.

Clarithromycin.indication: Respiratory tract / ENT infections / H. pylori eradication
Clarithromycin.duration_note: 7-14 days; H. pylori: 14 days as part of triple therapy
Clarithromycin.notes: 7.5 mg/kg BD (max 500 mg BD). Available as 125 mg/5 mL, 250 mg/5 mL suspension. Bitter taste — mix with milk. H. pylori: combine with PPI + amoxicillin.

Cefdinir.indication: Otitis media / Pharyngitis / Sinusitis / Skin infections
Cefdinir.notes: 7 mg/kg BD or 14 mg/kg OD (max 600 mg/day). 6 months-12 years. Suspension 125 mg/5 mL. May cause red stools (harmless). Separate from iron supplements by 2h.

Penicillin V.duration_note: 10 days for GAS pharyngitis; lifelong for RF prophylaxis
Penicillin V.notes: <=27 kg: 250 mg BD for 10 days; >27 kg: 500 mg BD for 10 days. RF prophylaxis: 250 mg BD continuous. Give on empty stomach.

Naproxen.duration_note: JIA: ongoing under specialist; acute pain: 5-7 days
Naproxen.notes: 5 mg/kg BD (max 10 mg/kg/day or 1000 mg/day). >=2 years only. Give with food. Suspension 125 mg/5 mL available. Monitor renal/GI.

Diclofenac.duration_note: Short-term: 3-5 days for pain; JIA: ongoing
Diclofenac.notes: >=1 year: 0.3-1 mg/kg TDS (max 3 mg/kg/day or 150 mg/day). Oral or rectal. IV: 0.3-1 mg/kg OD-BD (max 150 mg/day). Give with food.

Ketorolac.duration_note: MAX 2 days IV/IM; MAX 5 days oral. Not for chronic pain.
Ketorolac.notes: >=1 year: 0.5 mg/kg IV/IM q6h (max 30 mg/dose, 90 mg/day). Oral: 0.25 mg/kg q6h. Max total duration 5 days all routes combined. Not for <1 year.

Colchicine.notes: <5 years: 0.5 mg/day; 5-10 years: 1 mg/day; >10 years: 1.5 mg/day. May divide into BD. Increase gradually to max 2 mg/day if unresponsive. Give with food to reduce GI side effects.

Methotrexate.duration_note: Long-term; continue at least 1 year after remission
Methotrexate.notes: 10-15 mg/m2 ONCE WEEKLY (NOT daily). SC preferred if >=15 mg/m2 (better bioavailability). Give folic acid 1 mg/day (skip MTX day). Monitor FBC, LFTs, renal function. DIFFERENT from cancer dosing.

Hydroxychloroquine.notes: 3-5 mg/kg/day OD (max 400 mg/day or 6.5 mg/kg/day). NOT for <31 kg (200 mg tablet cannot be split). Annual eye exam for retinal toxicity. Risk increases >5 mg/kg/day.

Azathioprine.notes: 2-2.5 mg/kg/day OD. Check TPMT before starting — reduce dose if intermediate metabolizer; avoid if deficient. Monitor FBC weekly x8 weeks then monthly. 6-TGN target 235-450 pmol. IBD maintenance: may use up to 3 mg/kg/day.

Sulfasalazine.duration_note: Long-term for IBD/JIA; start low, increase weekly
Sulfasalazine.notes: Start 1/4 maintenance dose, increase weekly over 4 weeks. IBD maintenance: 20-30 mg/kg/day; IBD acute: 40-60 mg/kg/day. JIA: 30-50 mg/kg/day BD. Contraindicated <2 years and G6PD deficiency. Give with food.

Ursodeoxycholic acid.duration_note: Long-term; duration depends on underlying condition
Ursodeoxycholic acid.notes: Cholestasis: 10-15 mg/kg/day BD-TDS. Biliary atresia post-Kasai: 20 mg/kg/day. Cystic fibrosis liver disease: 20 mg/kg/day. Give with food. Suspension 250 mg/5 mL available.

Metformin.duration_note: Long-term; titrate over weeks to maintenance dose
Metformin.notes: >=10 years only. Start 500 mg OD with food; increase by 500 mg/week to max 2000 mg/day (divided BD-TDS). XR formulations available for OD dosing. Monitor renal function. Hold before contrast procedures.

Isotretinoin.notes: >=12 years. Start 0.5 mg/kg/day for 4 weeks, then increase to 1 mg/kg/day. Target cumulative dose 120-150 mg/kg. ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION in pregnancy — iPLEDGE required. Monitor lipids, LFTs, FBC. Give with fatty meal for absorption.

Methylprednisolone.notes: IV pulse: 15-30 mg/kg (max 1 g) over 1-2 hours. Nephrotic: 30 mg/kg alt day x6 pulses. Lupus flare/vasculitis: 30 mg/kg x3 consecutive days. Monitor BP, glucose, electrolytes during infusion. Oral: 1-2 mg/kg/day for anti-inflammatory.
Oral: 1-2 mg/kg/day divided BD (max 60 mg/day). Give with food. Taper if >7 days.

Octreotide.duration_note: 2-5 days continuous infusion for variceal bleeding
Octreotide.notes: Bolus: 1-2 mcg/kg IV (max 50 mcg) then infusion 1-2 mcg/kg/hr (max 50 mcg/hr). For refractory bleeding may increase to 4 mcg/kg/hr. SC for chronic: 4-8 mcg/kg/day divided TDS.

TERLIPRESSIN.notes: Bolus: 10-20 mcg/kg IV q4-6h; OR continuous infusion 30 mcg/kg/day. Limited pediatric data — specialist use only. Monitor for ischemic complications.

IVIG.duration_note: Single infusion over 10-12 hours; within 10 days of onset
IVIG.notes: 2 g/kg single infusion over 10-24h. Give with high-dose aspirin (30-50 mg/kg/day). If IVIG-resistant: second dose 2 g/kg. Start slow and increase rate per protocol.

IVIG.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat if needed
IVIG.notes: 0.8-1 g/kg single dose (ASH guideline). Alternative: 0.4 g/kg/day x 5 days. For active hemorrhage.
0.4-0.6 g/kg every 3-4 weeks IV. Target trough IgG >5 g/L. Monitor for infusion reactions.

Clobetasol propionate 0.05%.duration_note: MAX 2 weeks continuous. NOT for face/genitals/flexures.
Clobetasol propionate 0.05%.notes: Super-potent (Class I). Thin layer BD. Max 50 g/week in adults; less in children (limit to affected area). NOT for face, groin, axillae. Max 2 consecutive weeks. Avoid occlusive dressings in children.

CLOBETASONE.duration_note: Step down to emollients when controlled
CLOBETASONE.notes: Moderate potency. Thin layer BD. Can use on face for short courses (5-7 days). Suitable for children >1 year.

Betamethasone dipropionate 0.05%.duration_note: Max 2 weeks on face/flexures; 4 weeks on body
Betamethasone dipropionate 0.05%.notes: Potent steroid. Thin layer BD. Not for face/flexures long-term. Use FTU guide for dosing. Step down to moderate potency when controlled.

Hydrocortisone 0.5-1% cream.duration_note: 7-14 days; can use on face/flexures for short courses
Hydrocortisone 0.5-1% cream.notes: Mildest topical steroid. Safe for face, groin, axillae in short courses. 0.5% for infants <3 months; 1% for older. Apply after emollient (30 min gap).

Hydrocortisone 1% cream.duration_note: 7-14 days; safe for face up to 5 days
Hydrocortisone 1% cream.notes: Mild potency. Thin layer BD. First-line topical steroid for mild eczema in children. OK for face/flexures short-term.

Hydrocortisone 2.5% cream.notes: Mild-moderate potency. Thin layer BD. Avoid prolonged use on face.

Mupirocin.notes: 2% ointment. Apply thin layer TDS for 5-7 days. Cover with dressing if needed. For nasal decolonization (MRSA): apply to each nostril BD-TDS x 5 days.

Clotrimazole 1% cream.duration_note: 2-4 weeks; continue 1 week after lesions clear
Clotrimazole 1% cream.notes: Apply thin layer BD-TDS. Candidal nappy rash: 7-14 days. Tinea corporis: 2-4 weeks. Tinea pedis: 4 weeks. Safe for all ages.

Ketoconazole 2% cream.notes: Thin layer BD. Tinea versicolor: OD x 14 days. Topical only — systemic ketoconazole not recommended in children (hepatotoxicity).

Ketoconazole 2% shampoo.duration_note: Use twice weekly for 2-4 weeks; maintenance once weekly
Ketoconazole 2% shampoo.notes: Lather on wet scalp, leave 3-5 minutes, rinse. Use twice weekly x 2-4 weeks. Maintenance: once weekly or fortnightly.

Miconazole.notes: Oral gel 2%: <2 yr: 1.25 mL QID; 2-6 yr: 2.5 mL QID; >6 yr: 5 mL QID. Apply after feeds, hold in mouth as long as possible. Topical cream: BD-TDS for cutaneous infections.

Pimecrolimus 1% cream.duration_note: Use until flare resolves; safe for long-term intermittent use
Pimecrolimus 1% cream.notes: >=2 years. Thin layer BD. Second-line for mild-moderate atopic dermatitis. Safe for face, eyelids, skin folds (steroid-sparing). Burning/stinging common initially. Black box warning: theoretical malignancy risk (use second-line).

Adapalene 0.1% gel.duration_note: Minimum 12 weeks for full effect; long-term maintenance
Adapalene 0.1% gel.notes: >=12 years. Pea-sized amount to entire face at bedtime. Avoid sun exposure — use sunscreen. Initial irritation is normal (improves over weeks). Less irritating than tretinoin.

Benzoyl peroxide 2.5-5%.notes: Start 2.5% OD (less irritating, equally effective). Apply at bedtime. May bleach fabrics. Start every other day if irritation occurs. 5% equally effective as 10% with less irritation.

Clindamycin 1% lotion.duration_note: Max 12 weeks monotherapy; always combine with benzoyl peroxide to reduce resistance
Clindamycin 1% lotion.notes: >=12 years. ALWAYS combine with benzoyl peroxide (reduces resistance). Thin layer BD. Do not use as monotherapy.

Clindamycin 1% + Benzoyl peroxide 5% gel.notes: >=12 years. Pea-sized amount to affected area OD (evening). Store in refrigerator. May bleach fabrics.

Azelaic acid 15-20% cream.notes: >=12 years. 15% gel or 20% cream. Apply thin layer BD. Tingling/burning is common initially. Also effective for post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.

CALAMINE LOTION.notes: Apply PRN for itch relief. Shake well before use. Safe for all ages. May dry skin with prolonged use — combine with emollients.

POVIDONE IODINE.indication: Antiseptic for wounds / Pre-surgical skin preparation
POVIDONE IODINE.duration_note: Until wound heals; avoid prolonged use on large areas
POVIDONE IODINE.notes: 5-10% solution/ointment. Avoid on large areas in neonates and infants (iodine absorption → thyroid effects). Avoid in thyroid disorders. Not for body cavities.

Zinc oxide ointment.duration_note: Apply at each nappy change until resolved
Zinc oxide ointment.notes: Thick layer at each nappy change. Barrier protection. Safe for neonates. Can combine with antifungal if candidal superinfection suspected.

Petrolatum barrier ointment.duration_note: Long-term maintenance; cornerstone of eczema care
Petrolatum barrier ointment.notes: Apply liberally and frequently, especially after bathing. First-line emollient for eczema. Apply 30 min before topical steroids. Safe from birth.

Petroleum jelly (Vaseline).notes: Apply as needed. Excellent occlusive barrier. Safe from birth. Low cost, widely available.

Tacrolimus.duration_note: Use until flare resolves then step down to twice weekly maintenance
Tacrolimus.notes: TOPICAL 0.03%: >=2 years for moderate-severe atopic dermatitis. Thin layer BD. 0.1%: >=16 years. Burning sensation common initially. Black box: use second-line. For ORAL tacrolimus (transplant/nephrotic): see separate systemic entry.

Nephrotic: 0.1 mg/kg/day divided BD; target trough 5-7 ng/mL. Transplant: 0.15-0.2 mg/kg/day; target trough per protocol. TDM essential — narrow therapeutic index. Children need higher mg/kg than adults.

Mycophenolate mofetil.notes: 600 mg/m2 BD (max 2 g/day). Alternative: 20-30 mg/kg/day divided BD. Suspension 200 mg/mL available. Monitor FBC (leukopenia), LFTs. ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION in pregnancy. Take on empty stomach.

Adalimumab.notes: >=2 yr JIA: 10-<15 kg: 10 mg q2w; 15-<30 kg: 20 mg q2w; >=30 kg: 40 mg q2w. SC injection. Screen for TB before starting. Monitor for infections.

Etanercept.notes: >=2 yr: 0.8 mg/kg SC once weekly (max 50 mg/week). Alternative: 0.4 mg/kg SC twice weekly. Screen for TB before starting.

Tocilizumab.notes: sJIA >=2 yr: <30 kg: 12 mg/kg IV q2w; >=30 kg: 8 mg/kg IV q2w. pJIA: 8-10 mg/kg IV q4w. Monitor LFTs, lipids, neutrophils. TB screening required.

Rituximab.duration_note: Usually 2 doses given 2 weeks apart; repeat course as needed
Rituximab.notes: 375 mg/m2 IV x 2 doses (2 weeks apart). Premedicate with paracetamol, antihistamine, methylprednisolone. Monitor CD19/CD20 counts. Specialist use only.

Omalizumab.indication: Severe allergic asthma / Chronic spontaneous urticaria
Omalizumab.notes: >=6 yr asthma: dose based on weight + baseline IgE (nomogram). 75-375 mg SC q2-4 weeks. CSU >=12 yr: 150-300 mg SC q4w. Carry epinephrine — anaphylaxis risk (0.1%).

Dupilumab.notes: >=6 months: 5-<15 kg: 200 mg q4w (no loading); 15-<30 kg: 300 mg q4w (no loading). 6-11 yr: <30 kg: 100 mg q2w or 300 mg q4w; >=30 kg: 200 mg q2w or 300 mg q4w. >=12 yr: <60 kg: 200 mg q2w (load 400 mg); >=60 kg: 300 mg q2w (load 600 mg).

Cholestyramine.indication: Pruritus secondary to cholestasis / Bile acid malabsorption
Cholestyramine.notes: 240 mg/kg/day divided BD-TDS. Give before breakfast (peak bile acid release). Separate from other medications by 1h before or 4-6h after. May impair fat-soluble vitamin absorption — supplement ADEK.

Pancreatic enzyme replacement (Pancrelip.duration_note: Lifelong for CF; give with every feed/meal
Pancreatic enzyme replacement (Pancrelip.notes: Infants: 2000-4000 lipase units per 120 mL formula/breast feed. Open capsule, mix beads with acidic food (applesauce). Do not crush/chew beads.

Pancreatic enzyme replacement (Pancrelip.duration_note: Lifelong for CF; adjust to stool output
Pancreatic enzyme replacement (Pancrelip.notes: 1000-2500 lipase units/kg/meal. Snacks: half meal dose. MAX 10,000 units/kg/day or 4000 units/g fat (fibrosing colonopathy risk). Swallow capsules whole; if unable, open and mix with acidic food.

ANTACID (ALUMINIUM HYDROXIDE+MAGNESIUM H.duration_note: Short-term symptomatic relief; investigate if >2 weeks
ANTACID (ALUMINIUM HYDROXIDE+MAGNESIUM H.notes: 6-12 years: 2.5-5 mL TDS-QID between meals and at bedtime. >12 years: 5-10 mL. Shake well. Separate from other medications by 2 hours.

SIMETHICONE+DILL OIL+FENNEL OIL.notes: Colicaid/Bonnisan-type preparation. Give after feeds. 0.3-0.5 mL TDS for infants <6 months; 0.5-1 mL TDS for >6 months. Simethicone is not absorbed.

GLYCEROL.notes: Infant size (1 g): <2 years. Child size (2 g): 2-12 years. Insert PR. Acts within 15-30 min. For occasional use only — not daily.

BACILLUS CLAUSII.duration_note: During and 5 days after antibiotic course
BACILLUS CLAUSII.notes: Enterogermina: 1 vial (2 billion spores) BD-TDS. Can take with antibiotics (spore-forming, resistant). Safe from neonatal period.

Saccharomyces boulardii.duration_note: 5-7 days for acute diarrhea; during antibiotic course for AAD prevention
Saccharomyces boulardii.notes: 250 mg (5 billion CFU) BD. ESPGHAN recommends for acute gastroenteritis and AAD prevention. Do NOT use in immunocompromised or central line patients (fungemia risk).

Probiotics (LGG).notes: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG >=10 billion CFU/day. ESPGHAN: strong recommendation for acute infectious diarrhea and AAD prevention.

Probiotics (S. boulardii).notes: Same as Saccharomyces boulardii entry. 250 mg BD (5 billion CFU/dose).

Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG.notes: >=10 billion CFU/day. ESPGHAN-recommended strain for acute diarrhea and AAD prevention.

Azithromycin 1% ophthalmic.duration_note: Day 1-2: 1 drop BD; Day 3-5: 1 drop OD
Azithromycin 1% ophthalmic.notes: >=1 year. 1 drop BD x 2 days then OD x 5 days. Short course advantage. Trachoma (WHO): single oral azithromycin 20 mg/kg preferred.

Azithromycin prophylaxis.indication: MAC prophylaxis / Pertussis PEP / CF lung
Azithromycin prophylaxis.duration_note: Pertussis PEP: 5 days; MAC prophylaxis: ongoing; CF: thrice weekly
Azithromycin prophylaxis.notes: Pertussis PEP: 10 mg/kg day 1 then 5 mg/kg OD days 2-5. MAC prophylaxis (HIV): 5 mg/kg OD (max 250 mg). CF anti-inflammatory: 10 mg/kg Mon/Wed/Fri (max 250 mg).

Gatifloxacin 0.5% ophthalmic.notes: >=1 year. Day 1-2: 1 drop q2h (up to 8x/day); Day 3-7: 1 drop QID. 4th-gen fluoroquinolone — broad spectrum.

Moxifloxacin (ophthalmic).notes: >=1 year (0.5%). 1 drop TDS x 7 days. No preservative in some formulations — better tolerated.

Cyclopentolate 1% ophthalmic.duration_note: Single use for refraction; may repeat in 5-10 min if needed
Cyclopentolate 1% ophthalmic.notes: >=3 months: 1% (1 drop, repeat in 5 min if needed). Neonates/premature: use 0.5% (risk of seizures with 1%). Effect lasts 6-24h. Apply punctal occlusion.

TROPICAMIDE.duration_note: Single use; wait 15-20 min for effect
TROPICAMIDE.notes: 0.5% or 1%. 1 drop, wait 15-20 min. May repeat once. Shorter-acting than atropine (4-6h). Safe from infancy.

Atropine 1% eye drops (penalization).duration_note: As directed by ophthalmologist; weekend or daily penalization protocols
Atropine 1% eye drops (penalization).notes: 1% in sound eye for amblyopia penalization. Protocols: daily or weekend-only (Sat-Sun). Apply punctal pressure. Watch for systemic effects (flushing, tachycardia).

Atropine 0.05% eye drops.notes: 0.01-0.05% concentration at bedtime. ATOM2 trial: 0.01% effective with minimal side effects. Slows myopia progression in 6-12 year olds.

FLUOROMETHOLONE.notes: 0.1% suspension. 1 drop QID, taper over 2 weeks. Lower IOP-raising potential than prednisolone. Monitor IOP if >2 weeks use. >=2 years.

LOTEPREDNOL ETABONATE.duration_note: 2 weeks for allergy; post-op: per ophthalmologist
LOTEPREDNOL ETABONATE.notes: 0.5% suspension. 1 drop QID. Soft steroid — lower risk of IOP elevation. Use for seasonal allergic conjunctivitis >=2 years.

CARBOXYMETHYLCELLULOSE (EYE DROPS).notes: 1 drop PRN (up to 6-8x/day). Preservative-free preferred for frequent use. Safe for all ages.

Artificial tears (preservative-free).notes: 1 drop PRN. Use preservative-free unit-dose vials for frequent use (>4x/day). Safe for all ages.

Natamycin.duration_note: 14-21 days minimum; may need longer for deep infections
Natamycin.notes: 5% suspension. 1 drop q4-6h initially (may use hourly in severe keratitis); taper based on clinical response. Shake well. First-line for filamentous fungal keratitis.

Proparacaine.duration_note: Single procedural use only; NEVER for self-administration at home
Proparacaine.notes: 0.5%. 1-2 drops before procedure. Onset 20 sec, lasts 15-20 min. For tonometry, foreign body removal. NEVER prescribe for home use (corneal toxicity).

Ciprofloxacin otic preparations.duration_note: 7-10 days; may extend to 14 for CSOM
Ciprofloxacin otic preparations.notes: >=1 year. 0.3% solution/suspension. 3-4 drops BD (or as ciprofloxacin-dexamethasone combination 4 drops BD). Warm to body temperature. Tragal pumping after instillation.

Ofloxacin 0.3% otic.indication: Otitis externa / CSOM / Otitis media with tubes
Ofloxacin 0.3% otic.notes: 1-12 years: 5 drops BD; >12 years: 10 drops BD. Safe with perforated TM (no ototoxicity). Warm to body temperature.

Neomycin/Polymyxin B/Hydrocortisone otic.duration_note: 7-10 days; AVOID if TM perforation suspected
Neomycin/Polymyxin B/Hydrocortisone otic.notes: 3-4 drops TDS-QID. CONTRAINDICATED with perforated TM (neomycin ototoxicity). Use fluoroquinolone otic if TM status uncertain.

Acetic acid 2% otic solution.duration_note: 5-7 days treatment; 2-3 drops after swimming for prevention
Acetic acid 2% otic solution.notes: 3-5 drops TDS. Acidifies ear canal. Safe with perforated TM. May sting initially. Prophylaxis: 2-3 drops after swimming.

Docusate sodium 1% drops.duration_note: 3-5 days before irrigation; or single use before syringing
Docusate sodium 1% drops.notes: Waxsol or equivalent. 3-5 drops BD for 3-5 nights. Lie with affected ear upward for 5-10 min. Soften before ear syringing.

Carbamide peroxide 6.5%.duration_note: Max 4 days; follow with irrigation if needed
Carbamide peroxide 6.5%.notes: Debrox or equivalent. 5-10 drops BD, tilt head 5 min. Foaming action loosens wax. >=12 years (or under physician guidance for younger). Avoid if TM perforated.

Sodium bicarbonate ear drops 5%.notes: 3-5 drops BD x 3-5 days. Lie with affected ear up for 5-10 min. Safe for all ages. First-line cerumenolytic in UK.

Polymyxin B-Trimethoprim drops.notes: >=2 months. 1 drop q3-6h (max 6 doses/day) x 7-10 days. Good first-line for non-severe bacterial conjunctivitis.

FRAMYCETIN.notes: Soframycin cream/ointment 1%. Thin layer BD-TDS. Not for large/deep wounds. Aminoglycoside — avoid on large areas (systemic absorption).

NEOMYCIN+POLYMYXIN B+BACITRACIN ZINC.indication: Minor wounds / Burns / Skin infection prophylaxis
NEOMYCIN+POLYMYXIN B+BACITRACIN ZINC.notes: Neosporin ointment. Thin layer TDS. For minor cuts, scrapes, burns. Not for deep wounds. Neomycin allergy: use mupirocin instead.

NEOMYCIN+POLYMYXIN B+BACITRACIN+HYDROCOR.notes: Thin layer TDS x 7 days max. Do not use on face. Steroid component — risk of skin atrophy with prolonged use.

BETAMETHASONE+CLIOQUINOL.indication: Infected eczema / Fungal-bacterial mixed skin infections
BETAMETHASONE+CLIOQUINOL.notes: Potent steroid + antimicrobial. Thin layer BD. Stains clothing yellow. Max 7 days. Not for nappy area or face.

BETAMETHASONE+NEOMYCIN.indication: Infected eczema / Dermatitis with secondary infection
BETAMETHASONE+NEOMYCIN.notes: Potent steroid + aminoglycoside. Thin layer BD. Max 7 days. Avoid face/flexures. Check neomycin allergy.

CLOTRIMAZOLE+BECLOMETHASONE.duration_note: 2 weeks; then switch to plain antifungal
CLOTRIMAZOLE+BECLOMETHASONE.notes: Thin layer BD x 2 weeks. Then continue plain clotrimazole 1-2 more weeks. Steroid reduces inflammation early.

CLOBETASONE+MICONAZOLE.indication: Fungal skin infections with moderate inflammation
CLOBETASONE+MICONAZOLE.notes: Moderate potency steroid + antifungal. Thin layer BD. Then switch to antifungal alone.

CLOBETASOL+NEOMYCIN+MICONAZOLE.notes: Super-potent steroid + antibiotic + antifungal. MAX 7 days. NOT for face/groin. Switch to milder combination after 7 days.

FUSIDIC ACID+HYDROCORTISONE.duration_note: Max 7 days; then plain emollient or steroid alone
FUSIDIC ACID+HYDROCORTISONE.notes: Fucidin H. Thin layer BD x 7 days. Mild steroid + anti-staphylococcal. OK for face short-term. Max 7 days to reduce fusidic acid resistance.

BENZALKONIUM CHLORIDE+ZINC OXIDE (CREAM).indication: Nappy rash / Skin barrier with antiseptic
BENZALKONIUM CHLORIDE+ZINC OXIDE (CREAM).duration_note: At each nappy change until resolved
BENZALKONIUM CHLORIDE+ZINC OXIDE (CREAM).notes: Thick layer at each nappy change. Barrier + mild antiseptic. Safe from birth.

Entecavir.notes: >=2 years and >=10 kg. 0.015 mg/kg OD (max 0.5 mg). Oral solution 0.05 mg/mL. Take on empty stomach. Monitor HBV DNA and ALT.

Tenofovir disoproxil.notes: >=2 years and >=10 kg. 8 mg/kg OD (max 300 mg). Oral powder 40 mg/scoop available. Monitor renal function and bone density. Avoid with nephrotoxic drugs.

Sofosbuvir/Velpatasvir.notes: >=6 yr: 17-<30 kg: 200/50 mg OD; >=30 kg: 400/100 mg OD (adult dose). Pangenotypic. 12 weeks. Check SVR12 post-treatment.

OXYTOCIN.duration_note: Single dose at delivery; or IV infusion for induction
OXYTOCIN.notes: PPH prevention: 10 IU IM after delivery of anterior shoulder. Induction: 1-4 mU/min IV, increase q30 min. Obstetric use only.

CARBETOCIN.notes: 100 mcg IV bolus over 1 min after delivery at CS. Long-acting oxytocin analogue. Single dose only. Heat-stable formulation (WHO essential medicine).

CARBOPROST.duration_note: May repeat q15-90 min (max 8 doses / 2 mg total)
CARBOPROST.notes: 250 mcg deep IM. May repeat q15-90 min. Max 8 doses (2 mg total). CONTRAINDICATED in asthma. For oxytocin-refractory PPH.

MISOPROSTOL.duration_note: Single dose oral; or 800 mcg sublingual for PPH treatment
MISOPROSTOL.notes: PPH prevention: 600 mcg PO. PPH treatment: 800 mcg SL. Obstetric use only. WHO recommended where oxytocin unavailable.

DROTAVERINE.notes: 6-12 years: 10-20 mg BD; >12 years: 40 mg BD-TDS. No-Spa brand. Non-anticholinergic antispasmodic (PDE4 inhibitor). Limited pediatric data.

HYOSCINE BUTYLBROMIDE.duration_note: Short-term for colic; max 3 days without medical review
HYOSCINE BUTYLBROMIDE.notes: >=6 years: 10 mg TDS (max 6 tablets/day). Buscopan brand. Poorly absorbed orally — acts locally in GI tract. IV/IM: 0.3-0.6 mg/kg/day in divided doses.

VALETHEMATE.duration_note: Single use in labor; max 3 doses
VALETHEMATE.notes: Epidosin. 8 mg IV q1h (max 3 doses) during active labor to facilitate cervical dilation. Obstetric use only.

B-COMPLEX.duration_note: 1-3 months for deficiency; ongoing if dietary intake inadequate
B-COMPLEX.notes: <6 yr: 2.5 mL OD; >=6 yr: 5 mL OD-BD. Multiple formulations available. Check individual B vitamin levels if specific deficiency suspected.

Vitamin E.duration_note: Until levels normalize; longer in malabsorption
Vitamin E.notes: Deficiency treatment: 1 IU/kg/day for infants; 100-200 IU/day for children; up to 400 IU/day. CF/cholestasis: higher doses needed (5-10 IU/kg/day).

FERROUS ASCORBATE + FOLIC ACID.duration_note: 3 months treatment + 1-2 months after Hb normalizes to replete stores
FERROUS ASCORBATE + FOLIC ACID.notes: 3-6 mg elemental Fe/kg/day divided BD. Ferrous ascorbate has better absorption than ferrous sulfate. Give on empty stomach with vitamin C. Black stools are normal.

MULTIVITAMIN+MULTIMINERAL+AMINO ACID.notes: <6 yr: 2.5 mL OD; >=6 yr: 5 mL OD-BD. Multiple branded formulations. Give with food.

NEOGADINE ELIXIR (MULTIVITAMIN + MULTIMI.notes: <6 yr: 2.5 mL BD; >=6 yr: 5 mL BD. Contains digestive enzymes + multivitamins. Give before meals.

LIQUID PARAFFIN.duration_note: Short-term use; risk of lipoid pneumonia if aspirated
LIQUID PARAFFIN.notes: 3-6 yr: 5 mL at bedtime; 6-12 yr: 10 mL; >12 yr: 15-30 mL. AVOID in <3 years and children with swallowing difficulties (aspiration risk → lipoid pneumonia). Give at bedtime, not with meals.

LIQUID PARAFFIN+MILK OF MAGNESIA.duration_note: Short-term; prefer PEG 3350 for chronic constipation
LIQUID PARAFFIN+MILK OF MAGNESIA.notes: Cremaffin/equivalent. 3-6 yr: 5 mL at bedtime; 6-12 yr: 10 mL; >12 yr: 15-30 mL. Same aspiration precautions as liquid paraffin. Avoid <3 years.

Fortified tobramycin 1.4% + cefazolin 5%.duration_note: Hourly initially; taper over 2-3 weeks based on response
Fortified tobramycin 1.4% + cefazolin 5%.notes: Compounded preparation. Initial: alternating drops q30 min-1h around the clock. Taper to q2h then QID over 2-3 weeks. Specialist ophthalmology use only.

Potassium iodide.duration_note: Radiation: single dose or until exposure ceases; Sporotrichosis: 3-6 months
Potassium iodide.notes: Radiation emergency: 1 month-3 yr: 32 mg; 3-12 yr: 65 mg; >12 yr: 130 mg. Thyroid storm: 5 drops SSKI TDS (start 1h after antithyroid drug). Sporotrichosis: 5 drops TDS, increase by 1 drop/day.

Budesonide (oral viscous).duration_note: 12 weeks induction; then lowest effective maintenance dose
Budesonide (oral viscous).notes: Mix nebulizer respules with 5 g sucralose (Splenda) to make viscous slurry. Swallow — do not inhale. <10 yr: 0.5-1 mg BD; >=10 yr: 1-2 mg BD. No eating/drinking 30 min after.

Fluticasone (swallowed).notes: Swallow MDI puffs (do not inhale). <12 yr: 88-440 mcg BD; >=12 yr: 440-880 mcg BD. No spacer. No eating/drinking 30 min after. Alternative to oral viscous budesonide.

Lidocaine (viscous).indication: Oral mucositis / Mouth ulcers pain relief
Lidocaine (viscous).duration_note: As needed for pain; watch for systemic toxicity
Lidocaine (viscous).notes: 2% viscous: apply/swish and spit (older children) or dab on lesion. Max 4.5 mg/kg/dose q3-6h. AVOID in infants (swallowing risk → seizures). Monitor for numbness, dysphagia.

Peppermint oil (enteric-coated).indication: Irritable bowel syndrome / Functional abdominal pain
Peppermint oil (enteric-coated).notes: Enteric-coated capsules: >=8 yr: 0.1-0.2 mL TDS before meals. Swallow whole — do not crush. Heartburn if coating breached. Some evidence for IBS in children.

Selenium sulfide 2.5%.notes: Shampoo/lotion. Lather, leave 5-10 min, rinse. Tinea versicolor: apply to body, leave 10 min, rinse OD x 7 days. Scalp: twice weekly x 4 weeks.

Ciclopirox 1% cream/shampoo.duration_note: 2-4 weeks for skin; shampoo twice weekly
Ciclopirox 1% cream/shampoo.notes: >=10 years. Cream: thin layer BD x 2-4 weeks. Shampoo 1%: twice weekly. Broad antifungal spectrum.

Ciclopirox 8% nail lacquer.notes: Apply to affected nails OD. Remove with alcohol weekly before reapplication. Low efficacy as monotherapy (~10% cure). Consider oral terbinafine for moderate-severe.

Zinc pyrithione 1% shampoo.duration_note: Use 2-3 times weekly; long-term maintenance OK
Zinc pyrithione 1% shampoo.notes: Lather, leave 3-5 min, rinse. 2-3 times weekly. Maintenance: once weekly. Safe for children >=2 years.

Calcipotriene 0.005% cream.duration_note: 8 weeks; may use long-term with monitoring
Calcipotriene 0.005% cream.notes: >=2 years. Thin layer BD. Vitamin D analogue. Max 45 g/week (adults); proportionally less in children. Monitor serum calcium if large area. Avoid face.

Hydroquinone 2-4% cream.duration_note: Max 5-6 months then take 2-3 month break
Hydroquinone 2-4% cream.notes: >=12 years. Thin layer at bedtime to pigmented areas only. Use sunscreen daily. Not for large areas. Risk of ochronosis with prolonged use >6 months. Start 2%, increase to 4% if needed.

Hydrogen peroxide 1.5% rinse.notes: Swish and spit (not swallow). >=6 years only (must be able to spit). Dilute to 1.5% if using 3% solution. Max 7 days.

Chlorhexidine gluconate 0.12%.indication: Oral infections / Gingivitis / Mouth ulcer prophylaxis
Chlorhexidine gluconate 0.12%.notes: Swish 30 seconds, spit. BD after brushing (wait 30 min after toothpaste). >=6 years (must be able to spit). Causes brown staining of teeth (reversible).

Anhydrous lanolin.notes: Apply PRN as emollient. Lanolin allergy possible (~1%). Safe for neonates. Also used for breastfeeding nipple care.

Ruxolitinib cream 1.5%.duration_note: 8 weeks; may continue if benefit outweighs risk
Ruxolitinib cream 1.5%.notes: >=12 years. Thin layer BD to max 20% BSA. JAK inhibitor cream. Black box warning (class effect). Not first-line. Max 60 g/week.

Sarecycline.notes: >=9 years. 33-54 kg: 60 mg OD; 55-84 kg: 100 mg OD; 85-136 kg: 150 mg OD. Narrow-spectrum tetracycline. Less GI/photosensitivity vs doxycycline.

Diltiazem (topical).notes: 2% ointment (compounded). Pea-sized amount BD to perianal area. Relaxes internal anal sphincter. May cause local headache. Used alongside laxatives.

Ertapenem.duration_note: 5-14 days depending on indication and response
Ertapenem.notes: 3 months-12 yr: 15 mg/kg BD (max 1 g/day). >=13 yr: 1 g OD. IV over 30 min or IM. Once-daily carbapenem (in adolescents). Does NOT cover Pseudomonas or Acinetobacter.

Pivmecillinam.notes: 20-40 mg/kg/day divided TDS. Good for ESBL-producing E. coli UTIs (oral option). Swallow whole with food/water. Monitor for carnitine depletion with prolonged use.

IV pamidronate.duration_note: Given over 3 days; repeat every 2-4 months
IV pamidronate.notes: OI: 0.5-1 mg/kg/day x 3 days, repeated q2-4 months (cumulative 9 mg/kg/year). Infuse over 4h. Supplement calcium and vitamin D. Monitor Ca, PO4, renal function.

IV zoledronic acid.notes: 0.025-0.05 mg/kg IV over >=30 min. Repeat q6 months. Pre-hydrate. Flu-like symptoms common after first dose. Supplement calcium and vitamin D.

Risedronate.notes: 4-<10 yr: 15 mg once weekly; >=10 yr: 35 mg once weekly. Take on empty stomach with plain water. Remain upright 30 min. Limited pediatric data.

Denosumab.indication: Giant cell tumor of bone / Severe osteoporosis (specialist)
Denosumab.duration_note: Specialist use; risk of rebound hypercalcemia on discontinuation
Denosumab.notes: Giant cell tumor >=12 yr and skeletally mature: 120 mg SC q4 weeks (with loading on days 8, 15). CRITICAL: rebound hypercalcemia if stopped abruptly. Specialist use only.

Apremilast.notes: Limited pediatric data. Titration over 5 days (10mg OD → 10mg BD → 20mg OD+10mg PM → 20mg BD → 30mg BD). PDE4 inhibitor. Monitor weight (risk of weight loss).

Triamcinolone.duration_note: Max 2 weeks for topical; oral paste as needed for ulcers
Triamcinolone.notes: 0.025-0.1% cream/ointment: moderate-potent steroid. BD x max 2 weeks. Oral paste (Kenolog in Orabase) 0.1%: apply to ulcer BD-TDS. IM depot (40 mg): specialist use only.

Fluocinonide.notes: 0.05% cream/ointment. Potent (high) steroid. Thin layer BD. Max 2 consecutive weeks. Not for face, groin, axillae.

Abatacept.duration_note: Long-term; IV q4 weeks (with loading at weeks 0, 2, 4)
Abatacept.notes: IV >=6 yr: <75 kg: 10 mg/kg; 75-100 kg: 750 mg; >100 kg: 1000 mg. Weeks 0, 2, 4 then q4 weeks. SC >=2 yr: 10-<25 kg: 50 mg/wk; 25-50 kg: 87.5 mg/wk; >50 kg: 125 mg/wk.

Anakinra.notes: >=8 months (CAPS) or >=2 yr (sJIA): 1-2 mg/kg SC OD. MAS: may increase to 4-10 mg/kg/day. Injection site reactions common. Store refrigerated.

Canakinumab.notes: sJIA >=2 yr: 4 mg/kg SC q4 weeks (max 300 mg). CAPS >=2 yr: 2 mg/kg SC q8 weeks (15-40 kg); 150 mg q8 weeks (>40 kg). IL-1beta inhibitor.

Belimumab.notes: >=5 yr: 10 mg/kg IV at weeks 0, 2, 4, then q4 weeks. Infuse over 1h. Anti-BLyS monoclonal antibody.

Ustekinumab.duration_note: Long-term; SC injection every 12 weeks after loading
Ustekinumab.notes: Psoriasis >=6 yr: <60 kg: 0.75 mg/kg at weeks 0, 4 then q12w; 60-100 kg: 45 mg; >100 kg: 90 mg. IL-12/23 inhibitor.

Tofacitinib.notes: >=2 yr JIA: 10-<20 kg: 3.2 mg BD (oral solution); 20-<40 kg: 4 mg BD; >=40 kg: 5 mg BD (tablet). JAK inhibitor. Black box warnings: thrombosis, malignancy, infection.

Voclosporin.notes: 23.7 mg BD (3 capsules of 7.9 mg). Calcineurin inhibitor. Limited pediatric data — primarily adult indication. With MMF and low-dose steroid. Monitor renal function, BP, lipids.

Rilonacept.notes: >=12 yr: Loading 4.4 mg/kg SC (max 320 mg, split 2 sites), then 2.2 mg/kg weekly (max 160 mg). IL-1 trap. Limited pediatric data.

Emapalumab.duration_note: Until HSCT; escalate dose based on response
Emapalumab.notes: >=neonatal. Start 1 mg/kg IV every 3 days; increase to 3, 6, then 10 mg/kg q3 days if inadequate response. Anti-IFN-gamma. Bridge to HSCT. TB prophylaxis required.

Hyaluronidase-facilitated SCIG.notes: HyQvia. >=2 yr. Dose equivalent to prior IVIG dose (300-600 mg/kg q3-4 weeks). Infuse SC with hyaluronidase pre-injection. Single infusion site per session.

Glecaprevir/pibrentasvir (Mavyret).duration_note: 8-16 weeks depending on genotype and prior treatment
Glecaprevir/pibrentasvir (Mavyret).notes: >=3 yr: 12-<20 kg: 150/60 mg OD; 20-<30 kg: 200/80 mg OD; 30-<45 kg: 250/100 mg OD; >=45 kg: 300/120 mg OD. Take with food. 8 weeks treatment-naive.

Ledipasvir/sofosbuvir (Harvoni).duration_note: 12 weeks standard; 8 weeks possible for some genotype 1
Ledipasvir/sofosbuvir (Harvoni).notes: >=3 yr: <17 kg: 33.75/150 mg OD; 17-<35 kg: 45/200 mg OD; >=35 kg: 90/400 mg OD. Oral pellets or tablets. 12 weeks. Check SVR12.

Bismuth subsalicylate.indication: Acute diarrhea / H. pylori (quadruple therapy component)
Bismuth subsalicylate.duration_note: Max 2 days for acute diarrhea; 14 days for H. pylori
Bismuth subsalicylate.notes: NOT recommended <12 years (salicylate — Reye syndrome risk). >=12 yr: 262-524 mg QID. H. pylori: as part of quadruple therapy x 14 days. Black stools/tongue are harmless.

Botulinum toxin A injection.duration_note: Repeat every 3-6 months; specialist use only
Botulinum toxin A injection.notes: Spasticity >=2 yr: 1-6 U/kg per muscle (max 400 U total per session). NOT interchangeable between brands (Botox vs Dysport — different units). Specialist use only.

Botulinum toxin injection.notes: Same as Botulinum toxin A injection entry. 1-6 U/kg per muscle. Max 400 U per session. Specialist use only.
